title: Missing Person Mom
tagline: Love lives here. So does arson.
theme: Some homes hold families. Some hold motive.

beat_sheet.text: Derek Marlowe relocates to Blackthorn Lake and a heavily windowed fresh-start house after an emotionally negligent implosion, then pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting old family office described as retreat supplies. The starter engine here is: A child goes missing, mother knows something is off, and everybody ignores her for seventy minutes. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Tess Sutton hesitates before explaining marina tackle room described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. The ceramic owl is back on the kitchen island.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Brooke Hale is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Derek Marlowe says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Marigold Falls.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Brooke?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Evelyn. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Derek. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The ceramic owl is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Luke."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "Missing Person Mom understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "Missing Person Mom" follows Derek Marlowe, a recovery protagonist with paperwork who thinks a fresh start, a large house, and professional competence can outpace flawless suburban restart under hostile observation until Brooke Hale collides with her life carrying portable suspicion, plausible access to offsite storage, and the kind of calm that sounds premeditated in a dim kitchen. The governing injury is simple: A child goes missing, mother knows something is off, and everybody ignores her for seventy minutes. The signature tell is always this: The county telegraphs the problem once and nobody listens until act three. As a pool gate standing open where nobody remembers a gate, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home, every pool is a witness, and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Love lives here. So does arson.
